# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI pipeline with multiple stages where build artifacts are passed between jobs. The build job creates artifacts (binaries, configs) and deployment jobs download and execute them. Store artifacts for 30 days for team access.

Write secure code.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - build
  - test
  - staging
  - production

variables:
  SECURE_FILES_DOWNLOAD_PATH: "./secure-files"
  BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR: "build-output"
  BINARY_NAME: "myapp"

default:
  before_script:
    - echo "Pipeline ID ${CI_PIPELINE_ID} - Job ${CI_JOB_NAME}"

build:compile:
  stage: build
  image: golang:1.21-alpine
  script:
    - mkdir -p "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin"
    - mkdir -p "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/configs"
    - mkdir -p "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/migrations"
    - echo "Building application binary..."
    - CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build
        -ldflags="-s -w -X main.version=${CI_COMMIT_TAG:-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}} -X main.buildTime=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
        -trimpath
        -o "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin/${BINARY_NAME}"
        ./cmd/server
    - chmod 0755 "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin/${BINARY_NAME}"
    - sha256sum "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin/${BINARY_NAME}" > "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin/${BINARY_NAME}.sha256"
    - echo "Copying configuration files..."
    - cp -r configs/*.yaml "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/configs/" 2>/dev/null || echo "No yaml configs found"
    - cp -r configs/*.json "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/configs/" 2>/dev/null || echo "No json configs found"
    - cp -r migrations/ "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/migrations/" 2>/dev/null || echo "No migrations found"
    - |
      cat > "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/build-metadata.json" <<EOF
      {
        "pipeline_id": "${CI_PIPELINE_ID}",
        "commit_sha": "${CI_COMMIT_SHA}",
        "commit_short_sha": "${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}",
        "commit_ref": "${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME}",
        "tag": "${CI_COMMIT_TAG}",
        "build_date": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
        "built_by": "${GITLAB_USER_LOGIN}"
      }
      EOF
    - echo "Build artifacts created successfully"
    - ls -laR "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/"
  artifacts:
    name: "${CI_PROJECT_NAME}-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}-build"
    paths:
      - "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin/"
      - "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/configs/"
      - "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/migrations/"
      - "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/build-metadata.json"
    expire_in: 30 days
    when: on_success
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_TAG

build:docker:
  stage: build
  image: docker:24-dind
  services:
    - docker:24-dind
  needs:
    - job: build:compile
      artifacts: true
  variables:
    DOCKER_TLS_CERTDIR: "/certs"
    IMAGE_TAG: "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
    IMAGE_TAG_LATEST: "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:latest"
  script:
    - echo "Verifying binary integrity..."
    - cd "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin"
    - sha256sum -c "${BINARY_NAME}.sha256"
    - cd "${CI_PROJECT_DIR}"
    - echo "Building Docker image..."
    - docker login -u "${CI_REGISTRY_USER}" -p "${CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD}" "${CI_REGISTRY}"
    - docker build
        --no-cache
        --pull
        --build-arg BINARY_PATH="${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin/${BINARY_NAME}"
        --build-arg CONFIG_PATH="${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/configs"
        --label "org.opencontainers.image.created=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
        --label "org.opencontainers.image.revision=${CI_COMMIT_SHA}"
        --label "org.opencontainers.image.source=${CI_PROJECT_URL}"
        -t "${IMAGE_TAG}"
        -f Dockerfile .
    - docker push "${IMAGE_TAG}"
    - |
      if [ -n "${CI_COMMIT_TAG}" ]; then
        docker tag "${IMAGE_TAG}" "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_TAG}"
        docker push "${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_TAG}"
      fi
    - echo "${IMAGE_TAG}" > docker-image-tag.txt
  artifacts:
    name: "${CI_PROJECT_NAME}-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}-docker-info"
    paths:
      - docker-image-tag.txt
    expire_in: 30 days
    when: on_success
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_TAG

test:unit:
  stage: test
  image: golang:1.21-alpine
  needs:
    - job: build:compile
      artifacts: true
  script:
    - echo "Verifying build artifacts exist..."
    - test -f "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin/${BINARY_NAME}"
    - sha256sum -c "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin/${BINARY_NAME}.sha256"
    - echo "Running unit tests..."
    - go test -race -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic ./...
    - go tool cover -func=coverage.out
  artifacts:
    name: "${CI_PROJECT_NAME}-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}-test-results"
    paths:
      - coverage.out
    expire_in: 30 days
    reports:
      coverage_report:
        coverage_format: cobertura
        path: coverage.xml
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_TAG

test:integration:
  stage: test
  image: golang:1.21-alpine
  needs:
    - job: build:compile
      artifacts: true
  services:
    - name: postgres:15-alpine
      alias: test-db
  variables:
    POSTGRES_DB: testdb
    POSTGRES_USER: testuser
    POSTGRES_PASSWORD: "${TEST_DB_PASSWORD}"
    POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD: "scram-sha-256"
    POSTGRES_INITDB_ARGS: "--auth-host=scram-sha-256"
    DATABASE_URL: "postgres://testuser:${TEST_DB_PASSWORD}@test-db:5432/testdb?sslmode=disable"
  script:
    - echo "Verifying build artifacts..."
    - test -f "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin/${BINARY_NAME}"
    - sha256sum -c "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin/${BINARY_NAME}.sha256"
    - echo "Running integration tests..."
    - go test -tags=integration -race -timeout 300s ./...
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_TAG

test:security-scan:
  stage: test
  image: golang:1.21-alpine
  needs:
    - job: build:compile
      artifacts: true
  script:
    - echo "Running security scan on binary..."
    - test -f "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin/${BINARY_NAME}"
    - go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest
    - govulncheck ./...
    - echo "Checking for hardcoded secrets in configs..."
    - |
      SECRETS_FOUND=0
      for config_file in ${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/configs/*; do
        if [ -f "$config_file" ]; then
          if grep -iE '(password|secret|api_key|token|private_key)\s*[:=]\s*["\x27]?[a-zA-Z0-9+/=]{8,}' "$config_file"; then
            echo "WARNING: Potential secret found in $config_file"
            SECRETS_FOUND=1
          fi
        fi
      done
      if [ "$SECRETS_FOUND" -eq 1 ]; then
        echo "ERROR: Hardcoded secrets detected in configuration files!"
        exit 1
      fi
  artifacts:
    name: "${CI_PROJECT_NAME}-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}-security-report"
    paths:
      - security-report.json
    expire_in: 30 days
    when: always
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_TAG

deploy:staging:
  stage: staging
  image: alpine:3.18
  needs:
    - job: build:compile
      artifacts: true
    - job: build:docker
      artifacts: true
    - job: test:unit
      artifacts: false
    - job: test:security-scan
      artifacts: false
  environment:
    name: staging
    url: https://staging.${CI_PROJECT_NAME}.example.com
    on_stop: deploy:staging:stop
  variables:
    DEPLOY_ENV: staging
    KUBECONFIG_CONTENT: "${STAGING_KUBECONFIG}"
  before_script:
    - apk add --no-cache curl kubectl jq openssh-client
    - echo "Validating required artifacts..."
    - test -f "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin/${BINARY_NAME}"
    - test -f "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/build-metadata.json"
    - test -f docker-image-tag.txt
    - echo "Verifying binary integrity..."
    - sha256sum -c "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin/${BINARY_NAME}.sha256"
  script:
    - IMAGE_TAG=$(cat docker-image-tag.txt)
    - echo "Deploying ${IMAGE_TAG} to staging..."
    - echo "Build metadata:"
    - cat "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/build-metadata.json" | jq .
    - mkdir -p ~/.kube
    - echo "${KUBECONFIG_CONTENT}" | base64 -d > ~/.kube/config
    - chmod 0600 ~/.kube/config
    - |
      echo "Applying configuration files..."
      for config_file in ${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/configs/*; do
        if [ -f "$config_file" ]; then
          filename=$(basename "$config_file")
          echo "Creating/updating configmap for ${filename}..."
          kubectl create configmap "app-config-${filename%.*}" \
            --from-file="${config_file}" \
            --namespace="${DEPLOY_ENV}" \
            --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
        fi
      done
    - |
      echo "Running database migrations if present..."
      if [ -d "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/migrations" ] && [ "$(ls -A ${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/migrations 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
        echo "Migrations found, applying..."
        kubectl create configmap "app-migrations" \
          --from-file="${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/migrations/" \
          --namespace="${DEPLOY_ENV}" \
          --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
      fi
    - |
      echo "Updating deployment image..."
      kubectl set image deployment/myapp \
        myapp="${IMAGE_TAG}" \
        --namespace="${DEPLOY_ENV}"
    - |
      echo "Waiting for rollout to complete..."
      if ! kubectl rollout status deployment/myapp \
        --namespace="${DEPLOY_ENV}" \
        --timeout=300s; then
        echo "ERROR: Deployment rollout failed! Rolling back..."
        kubectl rollout undo deployment/myapp --namespace="${DEPLOY_ENV}"
        exit 1
      fi
    - echo "Staging deployment successful"
    - |
      echo "Running smoke tests..."
      STAGING_URL="https://staging.${CI_PROJECT_NAME}.example.com"
      HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" --max-time 30 "${STAGING_URL}/health")
      if [ "${HTTP_STATUS}" != "200" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: Health check failed with status ${HTTP_STATUS}"
        kubectl rollout undo deployment/myapp --namespace="${DEPLOY_ENV}"
        exit 1
      fi
      echo "Health check passed (HTTP ${HTTP_STATUS})"
  after_script:
    - rm -f ~/.kube/config
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_TAG

deploy:staging:stop:
  stage: staging
  image: alpine:3.18
  variables:
    GIT_STRATEGY: none
    KUBECONFIG_CONTENT: "${STAGING_KUBECONFIG}"
  environment:
    name: staging
    action: stop
  before_script:
    - apk add --no-cache kubectl
  script:
    - mkdir -p ~/.kube
    - echo "${KUBECONFIG_CONTENT}" | base64 -d > ~/.kube/config
    - chmod 0600 ~/.kube/config
    - kubectl scale deployment/myapp --replicas=0 --namespace=staging || true
  after_script:
    - rm -f ~/.kube/config
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH
      when: manual
      allow_failure: true

deploy:production:
  stage: production
  image: alpine:3.18
  needs:
    - job: build:compile
      artifacts: true
    - job: build:docker
      artifacts: true
    - job: deploy:staging
      artifacts: false
  environment:
    name: production
    url: https://${CI_PROJECT_NAME}.example.com
  variables:
    DEPLOY_ENV: production
    KUBECONFIG_CONTENT: "${PRODUCTION_KUBECONFIG}"
  before_script:
    - apk add --no-cache curl kubectl jq openssh-client
    - echo "Validating required artifacts..."
    - test -f "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin/${BINARY_NAME}"
    - test -f "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/build-metadata.json"
    - test -f docker-image-tag.txt
    - echo "Verifying binary integrity before production deploy..."
    - sha256sum -c "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/bin/${BINARY_NAME}.sha256"
  script:
    - IMAGE_TAG=$(cat docker-image-tag.txt)
    - echo "Deploying ${IMAGE_TAG} to production..."
    - echo "Build metadata:"
    - cat "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/build-metadata.json" | jq .
    - mkdir -p ~/.kube
    - echo "${KUBECONFIG_CONTENT}" | base64 -d > ~/.kube/config
    - chmod 0600 ~/.kube/config
    - |
      echo "Applying production configuration files..."
      for config_file in ${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/configs/*; do
        if [ -f "$config_file" ]; then
          filename=$(basename "$config_file")
          echo "Creating/updating configmap for ${filename}..."
          kubectl create configmap "app-config-${filename%.*}" \
            --from-file="${config_file}" \
            --namespace="${DEPLOY_ENV}" \
            --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
        fi
      done
    - |
      echo "Running database migrations if present..."
      if [ -d "${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/migrations" ] && [ "$(ls -A ${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/migrations 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
        echo "Migrations found, applying..."
        kubectl create configmap "app-migrations" \
          --from-file="${BUILD_OUTPUT_DIR}/migrations/" \
          --namespace="${DEPLOY_ENV}" \
          --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
      fi
    - |
      echo "Performing canary deployment..."
      kubectl set image deployment/myapp-canary \
        myapp="${IMAGE_TAG}" \
        --namespace="${DEPLOY_ENV}" 2>/dev/null || echo "No canary deployment found, proceeding with rolling update"
    - |
      echo "Updating main deployment image..."
      kubectl set image deployment/myapp \
        myapp="${IMAGE_TAG}" \
        --namespace="${DEPLOY_ENV}"
    - |
      echo "Waiting for production rollout to complete..."
      if ! kubectl rollout status deployment/myapp \
        --namespace="${DEPLOY_ENV}" \
        --timeout=600s; then
        echo "ERROR: Production deployment rollout failed! Rolling back..."
        kubectl rollout undo deployment/myapp --namespace="${DEPLOY_ENV}"
        exit 1
      fi
    - echo "Production deployment successful"
    - |
      echo "Running production smoke tests..."
      PROD_URL="https://${CI_PROJECT_NAME}.example.com"
      for i in 1 2 3; do
        HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" --max-time 30 "${PROD_URL}/health")
        if [ "${HTTP_STATUS}" = "200" ]; then
          echo "Health check ${i}/3 passed (HTTP ${HTTP_STATUS})"
        else
          echo "ERROR: Health check ${i}/3 failed with status ${HTTP_STATUS}"
          if [ "$i" -eq 3 ]; then
            echo "All health checks failed. Rolling back..."
            kubectl rollout undo deployment/myapp --namespace="${DEPLOY_ENV}"
            exit 1
          fi
          sleep 10
        fi
      done
      echo "All production health checks passed"
  after_script:
    - rm -f ~/.kube/config
  when: manual
  allow_failure: false
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_TAG =~ /^v\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH

deploy:rollback:production:
  stage: production
  image: alpine:3.18
  variables:
    GIT_STRATEGY: none
    KUBECONFIG_CONTENT: "${PRODUCTION_KUBECONFIG}"
  environment:
    name: production
  before_script:
    - apk add --no-cache kubectl
  script:
    - mkdir -p ~/.kube
    - echo "${KUBECONFIG_CONTENT}" | base64 -d > ~/.kube/config
    - chmod 0600 ~/.kube/config
    - echo "Rolling back production deployment..."
    - kubectl rollout undo deployment/myapp --namespace=production
    - kubectl rollout status deployment/myapp --namespace=production --timeout=300s
    - echo "Rollback completed successfully"
  after_script:
    - rm -f ~/.kube/config
  when: manual
  allow_failure: false
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_TAG =~ /^v\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/
